metadata.title: Claude Design.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Claude Design lives at its own URL, claude.ai/design, needs a paid plan, and burns tokens faster than regular Claude. In one two-line prompt it built a high-fidelity landing page that looked designed, generating both the copy and visual structure. The workflow is the Wireframe tab, High Fidelity, paste, Create, then Present; a Slide Deck tab builds pitch decks similarly. The prompt names funding goal, audience, product, and tone, plus two reference sites that give it the visual register.

artifactLines: Claude Design lives at its own URL, claude.ai/design, needs a paid plan, and burns tokens faster than regular Claude.
In one two-line prompt it built a high-fidelity landing page that looked designed, generating both the copy and visual structure.
The workflow is the Wireframe tab, High Fidelity, paste, Create, then Present; a Slide Deck tab builds pitch decks similarly.
The prompt names funding goal, audience, product, and tone, plus two reference sites that give it the visual register.

beats.narration_text: The detail that stuck with me is that two reference sites are what separate a generic layout from something that looks like it hired a designer. I want to run the landing-page test myself. For a fictional product of my own: (1) show me how to fill the prompt template's four slots — funding goal, investor audience, product, and tone — for my idea, (2) explain how to choose two reference sites that actually teach Claude the visual register I want, and (3) tell me when to switch from the Wireframe High Fidelity flow to the Slide Deck tab.

props.command: The detail that stuck with me is that two reference sites are what separate a generic layout from something that looks like it hired a designer. I want to run the landing-page test myself. For a fictional product of my own: (1) show me how to fill the prompt template's four slots — funding goal, investor audience, product, and tone — for my idea, (2) explain how to choose two reference sites that actually teach Claude the visual register I want, and (3) tell me when to switch from the Wireframe High Fidelity flow to the Slide Deck tab.

beats.narration_text: How to get in. Claude Design is not inside the Claude app. Go to claude.ai/design — that's its own URL. You need a paid plan: Pro or Max gets you in directly. Team or Enterprise is off by default — your admin must enable it under Organization settings, Capabilities, Anthropic Labs. It's a research preview, rolling out gradually. If the URL redirects you home, wait a few days. It uses your existing plan limits, and it burns tokens fast — faster than regular Claude.

artifactLines: Pro or Max plan → go to claude.ai/design. Sign in. Done.
Team or Enterprise → admin enables it: Org Settings → Capabilities → Anthropic Labs.
Research preview: gradual rollout. If it redirects home, wait.
Token usage: fast. Faster than regular Claude. Watch your limits.
Send to Canva button: built in at the end of every output.

beats.narration_text: First test: a landing page, two-line prompt, one shot. Claude Design can build a high-fidelity landing page for a fictional underwater datacenter company — targeted at Israeli VCs — in a single prompt. No iterations, no back-and-forth. The result looked designed. The model generated both the copy and visual structure. The workflow: Wireframe tab, select High Fidelity, paste the prompt, hit Create, then click Present.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B02.png (screenshot of the underwater-datacenter landing page output from Claude Design — the one-shot high-fidelity result from the article)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: First test: a landing page, two-line prompt, one shot. Claude Design can build a high-fidelity landing page for a fictional underwater datacenter company — targ

beats.narration_text: The landing page prompt template. It's designed to give Claude the context it needs to make something that looks intentional, not generic. You name the funding goal, the investor audience, the product, and the tone — then you give two reference sites so Claude understands the visual register. That last part is what separates a generic layout from something that looks like it hired a designer.

props.title: landing-page-prompt.md
props.code:
Create a high-fidelity landing page to raise
$[AMOUNT] from [TARGET INVESTORS] for
"[PRODUCT NAME]" — [short description]. Target audience: [investor type].
Tone: [how visitor should feel] — think [website] + [website] + [ecosystem]. # Workflow:
# 1. Wireframe tab → select High Fidelity
# 2. Paste prompt → Create
# 3. Click Present to see it full-screen
# 4. Comment mode to request edits

beats.narration_text: Second test: a slide deck. Same project — underwater datacenters — this time as a pitch deck for a sales team. Switch to the Slide Deck tab on the homepage. Paste the prompt, answer Claude's clarifying questions, and it builds the deck. The resulting deck had coherent design language, section breaks, and slides structured for a sales conversation, not a generic bullet list.

props.artifactHeading: Three steps from prompt to deck
artifactLines: 2. Paste the prompt. Answer Claude's clarifying questions.
3. Deck appears. Click Present. Export or Send to Canva.
Claude asks questions first — answer fast, first instinct wins.
Edit by clicking a slide and describing the change in plain English.

beats.narration_text: The advanced workflow is a two-stage handoff: Cowork runs the strategy, Design runs the output. In Cowork, generate the content — the narrative, the data points, the structure. Then take that output into Claude Design and build the visual layer on top of it. Two separate tools, one clean handoff. Cowork does the thinking; Design does the rendering. That's the workflow worth copying.

props.artifactHeading: Strategy in Cowork, visuals in Design
artifactLines: Step 1: Open Claude Cowork. Generate the full content.
Step 2: Copy the output. Go to claude.ai/design.
Step 3: Paste as context. Build the visual layer on top.
Result: designed output grounded in real strategic thinking.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Go to claude.ai/design right now. Thirty minutes, one real project. Pick something you have to make this week — a pitch, a landing page, a deck — and let Claude Design build it one shot. Then compare it to what you would have made. Paste this prompt as your starting point.

props.command: Create a high-fidelity landing page designed to raise $[AMOUNT] from [TARGET AUDIENCE] for "[PRODUCT]" — [description]. Tone: [feel] — think [reference site] + [reference site]. Make it one-shot, high fidelity, ready to present.
